text:
Once upon a time, in a quaint village full of strange birds, there lived an owl named Owley. Owley hadn. Owls don't talk much. But oh, his wings!

One moonlit night, Owley saw something unbelievable. A flock of golden eagles. He decided to follow them. They flew higher than ever.

At the peak of an ancient oak, they stopped. Eagles. Owls. Odd companions.

They gazed at moony stars. Then Owlsey felt something odd. Little tiny hooks.

He hovered. Something was snatching feathers.

Quickly!

He caught plume feathers. Owlsey knew.

Birds!

They chased away bats. Owlsey thought maybe he'd play the feather thief myself.

No. Too silly.

Home.

Maybe feathers find you. Owley thought.

You think?

Maybe I'll knit one.

They laugh. Friends.

Fathers love them. Owley thought.

Because feathers feel good.

True.

Sometimes he likes to just
